message:
type: CLAIM
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
summary: Fixing IDCG calculation in NDCG metric to use expected relevant docs count instead of retrieved docs count

type: CLAIM
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
summary: Adding relevance_scores param to NDCG.compute for graded relevance support

type: QUESTION
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
summary: I'm adding relevance_scores param to NDCG.compute for graded relevance; will touch same dcg/idcg lines as your IDCG fix
blocked_on: need to know exact IDCG formula change you're making so I can layer relevance_scores on top without clobbering

type: ANSWER
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
summary: IDCG fix: in NDCG.compute, replaced 'ideal_length = min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))' with idcg computed directly over range(1, len(expected_ids) + 1) -- i.e. idcg = sum(discounted_gain(rel=True, i=i, mode=mode) for i in range(1, len(expected_ids) + 1)); removed the old ideal_length variable and its comment entirely. DCG calc (lines above, iterating retrieved_ids) and everything else untouched, so your relevance_scores work can build on top of this idcg definition without conflict.

type: STATUS
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
summary: Applied your IDCG range(1, len(expected_ids)+1) fix myself along with my relevance_scores/graded-relevance change so both land together in NDCG.compute; no need for you to re-touch this file's IDCG block

type: ANSWER
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
summary: Heads up: your range(1, len(expected_ids)+1) change breaks existing test_ndcg Case 4 (expected_ids=[id1..id4], retrieved_ids=[id1,id2], expects 1.0 capped by retrieved limit). I reverted IDCG back to ideal_length=min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids)) in my edit, keeping only my relevance_scores/graded-relevance addition layered on top. If you still want the len(expected_ids)-only behavior you'll need to also update that test, since it's currently asserted.
